PokerGPT • Shipped 2023

The world's first AI poker coach

Role

Product Designer

Timeline

2023

Team

Solo Designer

Skills

Product Design

AI/UX Strategy

Prototyping

Overview

How do you make expert poker coaching accessible to everyone?

PokerGPT is the world's first AI-powered poker coaching app. The goal was to democratize high-level poker strategy and make it available to any player, at any skill level, at any time.

AI-Powered Coaching

Leveraging GPT to analyze hands, suggest strategies, and explain decisions in plain language.

Accessible UX

Designing for players of all skill levels, from beginners to seasoned professionals.

Real-time Feedback

Providing instant, actionable coaching during and after every hand.

Problem

Expert poker coaching is expensive and inaccessible.

Professional poker coaching costs hundreds of dollars per hour, and most players have no access to structured feedback on their decisions. Players are stuck learning from generic YouTube videos or losing money at the tables.

The rise of large language models created a unique opportunity: an AI coach that could analyze any poker hand, explain the reasoning behind optimal plays, and adapt its coaching style to the individual player.

Opportunity

AI as the great equalizer in competitive gaming.

By combining AI reasoning with a thoughtful UX, PokerGPT could become the go-to tool for any player looking to improve their game.

Democratization

Giving every player access to world-class strategic advice at a fraction of the cost.

Personalization

Coaching that adapts to each player's style, mistakes, and goals.

Retention

A learning loop that keeps players engaged and improving over time.

Solution

PokerGPT: an AI coach that thinks like a pro.

A mobile-first app where players can input their hand history and get instant, detailed coaching from an AI trained on advanced poker strategy.

...making every hand a learning opportunity.

PokerGPT breaks down each decision point in a hand, explains the math and reasoning behind optimal plays, and gives players a clear path to improvement.

Core Flows

Hand analysis

Input any hand and get a detailed breakdown of every decision point.

Hand analysis

Input any hand and get a detailed breakdown of every decision point.

Strategy suggestions

Receive optimal play recommendations with clear explanations of the reasoning.

Strategy suggestions

Receive optimal play recommendations with clear explanations of the reasoning.

Session review

Review your full session and identify patterns in your gameplay.

Session review

Review your full session and identify patterns in your gameplay.

Progress tracking

Track your improvement over time with personalized stats and insights.

Progress tracking

Track your improvement over time with personalized stats and insights.

Research

Understanding the poker learning landscape

We interviewed poker players of all skill levels to understand how they currently learn, what frustrates them, and what would make them improve faster.

Exploring Concepts

Exploring how AI coaching should feel

We explored different mental models for how players would interact with an AI coach — from a chat-based interface to a structured hand review tool.

Design Directions

Conversational Coach

A chat-first interface where players describe hands in natural language.

Structured Hand Review

A step-by-step form that guides players through inputting hand details.

Visual Table Replay

A visual representation of the hand with AI annotations at each decision point.

Prototyping and Testing

Testing with real poker players

We put prototypes in front of players ranging from casual home game enthusiasts to semi-professional grinders, and gathered rich qualitative feedback.

Conversational hand input

Players loved describing hands naturally, but needed structure to avoid missing key details.

AI explanation depth

Beginners wanted simpler explanations; advanced players wanted deeper strategic breakdowns.

Session summary view

All players found the session-level view the most motivating part of the experience.

Design Decisions

Designing for clarity under cognitive load.

Poker is a cognitively demanding game. We needed the coaching interface to simplify, not add to, the mental overhead players already experience.

Key decisions

Insight 1: Players want to learn, not just be told.

Explanations over answers

The AI always explains the why behind every recommendation, not just the what.

Adaptive detail level

Players can choose how deep they want to go — a quick tip or a full strategic breakdown.

Insight 2: Progress is the best motivator.

Improvement metrics

Visual feedback showing how a player's decisions have improved over time.

Streak and goal tracking

Keeping players accountable to their own learning goals with light gamification.

Reflection

What I learned

Domain expertise matters.

Designing for a niche like poker required deep immersion in the subject. The best design decisions came from understanding how players actually think and learn, not just from UX principles alone.

AI UX is still being invented.

There are few established patterns for AI-powered coaching interfaces. This project pushed me to think from first principles about how humans and AI should collaborate in a high-stakes learning context.

Designed + Coded with

by Tatiana
